images.caption: Figure 1:Overview and efficiency analysis of PerceptionDLM.(a)Given an image and multiple region masks, PerceptionDLM generates captions for all regions in parallel through a denoising diffusion process. In contrast to conventional AR-based models like DAM[lian2025describe]and GAR[wang2025grasp]that process regions sequentially, our approach entirely avoids the linear growth in inference cost.(b)Throughput (TPS) scaling comparison. PerceptionDLM achieves near-linear TPS growth with stable per-image latency as the number of region masks increases.(c)Parallelism scaling under a constant workload (4 masks per image), demonstrating up to a3.44×3.44\timesthroughput speedup when fully parallelized.

Figure 2:Overview of the proposed parallel region perception architecture.Built upon PerceptionDLM-Base, the model integrates region prompting, RoI-aligned feature replay, and a structured attention masking mechanism to enable parallel, disentangled caption generation for multiple regions within a single diffusion process.

header: 3Multimodal Diffusion Language Model for Parallel Perception

images.caption: Figure 3:The evaluation pipeline for detailed localized captioning in ParaDLC-Bench.(a) During evaluation, given an input image and multiple region masks, the Perception DLM simultaneously generates textual descriptions for all specified regions in a single pass. (b) Subsequently, each generated caption, paired with its corresponding evaluation questions, is fed into a text-only large language model (LLM Judge) to calculate the final score. (c) In the scoring mechanism, positive questions reward accurate details and penalize factual errors, while negative questions strictly penalize mislocalization and hallucinations to prevent false positives in irrelevant areas.
Figure 5:Qualitative examplesof PerceptionDLM generating detailed descriptions for multiple masked regions in parallel.

Figure 6:Qualitative comparison of PerceptionDLM against existing baselines.Correct attribute descriptions are highlighted in green, whereas hallucinations and errors are marked in red. PerceptionDLM effectively mitigates the severe cross-region interference observed in other models.
